The '''56th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], took place on 23 February 2003 at the [[Odeon Luxe Leicester Square|Odeon Leicester Square]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2002 in film|2002]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2002.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/2788297.stm|title=Stars arrive for Baftas|publisher=[[BBC News]]|date=23 February 2003 |access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref>

''[[The Pianist (2002 film)|The Pianist]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and Best Director for [[Roman Polanski]]. [[Daniel Day-Lewis]] won Best Actor for ''[[Gangs of New York]]'' and [[Nicole Kidman]] won Best Actress for ''[[The Hours (film)|The Hours]]''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2003/feb/24/filmawards.filmnews|title=Britain's big Bafta night as The Hours has the edge on Hollywood blockbusters|work=[[The Guardian]]|date=24 February 2003|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref> [[Christopher Walken]] won Best Supporting Actor for ''[[Catch Me If You Can]]'' and [[Catherine Zeta-Jones]] won Best Supporting Actress for ''[[Chicago (2002 film)|Chicago]]''. ''[[The Warrior (2001 British film)|The Warrior]]'', directed by [[Asif Kapadia]], was voted Outstanding British Film of 2002.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://edition.cnn.com/2003/SHOWBIZ/Movies/02/24/film.baftas.reut/|title='Pianist,' Kidman win BAFTAs|publisher=[[CNN International]]|date=24 February 2003|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref>

[[Stephen Fry]] hosted the ceremony for the second consecutive year.
